channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Joe Sibanyoni withdraws court application as extortion case proceeds
item.description: Mpumalanga taxi boss Joe Sibanyoni, also known as Joe “Ferrari”, and his co-accused have formally withdrawn their urgent High Court application that sought to stop the re-enrolment of their extortion ...

item.title: Cute Abiola apologises to Patience Jonathan over past ridicule of her emotional reactions to insecurity
item.description: Nigerian skit maker Cute Abiola don publicly apologise to former First Lady Patience Jonathan over how Nigerians, including himself, mocked her emotional reactions in the past when she dey speak about...

item.title: Domeng weakens as it moves north, but monsoon rains continue across Luzon and nearby areas
item.description: Typhoon Domeng (international name Jangmi) has slightly weakened while moving northward over the Philippine Sea, according to PAGASA. As of 11 a.m. on June 1, the storm was located about 710 kilometer...

item.title: UK and France intercept sanctioned Russian oil tanker in Atlantic joint operation
item.description: France and the United Kingdom have taken part in a joint maritime operation to intercept a tanker suspected of violating international sanctions on Russia, according to French President Emmanuel Macro...

item.title: Los Angeles mayoral candidates campaign across the city ahead of primary election
item.description: In the final stretch before Los Angeles’ primary election, the leading mayoral candidates—incumbent Karen Bass, City Councilmember Nithya Raman, and former reality TV personality Spencer Pratt—spent t...

item.title: KZN road safety operation arrests 13 motorists for drunk driving, including taxi driver 20 times over limit and SANDF member
item.description: A KwaZulu-Natal road safety enforcement operation in Ladysmith has resulted in the arrest of 13 motorists for driving under the influence of alcohol, including a taxi driver found to be 20 times over ...

item.title: ODM-aligned Linda Mwananchi faction plans major political rally in Thika to expand Central Kenya outreach
item.description: The ODM-aligned Linda Mwananchi faction has announced plans to hold a major political rally in Thika on 14 June as part of its broader nationwide mobilisation campaign ahead of Kenya’s 2027 General El...

item.title: Employment tribunal awards £149,000 to athlete after findings of unfair treatment and pay disputes at Nuffield Health
item.description: A former canoe polo champion and personal trainer, Beth Littlewood from Bridgend, has been awarded around £149,000 in compensation following a series of employment tribunal findings against Nuffield H...

item.title: India’s AI transition and the need to prioritise human capital for employment growth
item.description: The article discusses concerns around the growing impact of artificial intelligence (AI) on global employment, warning of what some economists describe as an “AI jobs apocalypse.” It argues that unlik...

item.title: Minister Aucamp Highlights Centenary and Future Stewardship of Kruger National Park
item.description: Minister of Forestry, Fisheries and the Environment, Willie Aucamp, addressed the centenary celebrations of Kruger National Park (KNP) at Skukuza Rest Camp in Mpumalanga, urging South Africans to embr...

item.title: US–Iran tensions escalate amid continued strikes and stalled ceasefire negotiations
item.description: The United States and Iran have exchanged military strikes over the weekend as diplomatic efforts to finalize a ceasefire agreement continue to stall. President Donald Trump has requested revisions to...

item.title: CBSE opens Class 12 answer sheet verification and re-evaluation portal at cbse.gov.in; application process begins
item.description: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has opened the Class 12 re-evaluation and verification portal on its official website cbse.gov.in from June 1, 2026. This facility allows students who a...

item.title: DCI arrests drug trafficking suspect carrying eight bags of bhang in Bomet operation
item.description: Detectives from the Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) in Bomet County have arrested a man suspected of drug trafficking following an intelligence-led operation along the Silibwet–Bomet Road...

item.title: Ditsong National Museum opens Regiments Room showcasing Johannesburg’s reserve force military heritage
item.description: A new exhibition space known as the Regiments Room has opened at the Ditsong National Museum of Military History in Saxonwold, Johannesburg, celebrating the city’s long-forgotten reserve force regimen...

item.title: EU Foreign Affairs Chief Kaja Kallas visits Pakistan for 8th Strategic Dialogue
item.description: European Union’s High Representative for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy, Kaja Kallas, arrived in Pakistan on Monday to participate in the 8th EU-Pakistan Strategic Dialogue. She will co-chair the...

item.title: AIU probes irregularities in recognition of university athletes
item.description: The Association of Indian Universities (AIU) is currently under scrutiny not only for alleged financial and administrative irregularities but also for serious issues regarding the certification of uni...

item.title: Middle Belt Tigers emerge inaugural champions of Nigeria Super League
item.description: The Middle Belt Tigers don make history as dem clinch the first-ever Nigeria Super League title after a thrilling six-run win against Southern Sharks at the TBS Cricket Oval in Lagos. Dem posted 104/5...

item.title: Scientists Investigate a Reversal in a Large Flow Region of Earth's Outer Core Detected in 2010
item.description: Researchers analyzing satellite measurements of Earth's magnetic field have identified an unusual event that occurred in 2010 within Earth's outer core. According to the study, a large region of molte...

item.title: Centre approves appointment of five Supreme Court judges following Collegium recommendation
item.description: The Central Government has approved the appointment of five new judges to the Supreme Court of India, significantly increasing the court’s working strength. Law Minister Arjun Ram Meghwal announced th...

item.title: Vietnamese President To Lam’s Scheduled Senate Visit and Meeting with Senate President Cayetano Called Off During Manila State Visit
item.description: Vietnam canceled the scheduled visit of President To Lam to the Philippine Senate as well as his planned meeting with Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano on June 1, 2026. According to the Senate’s Of...
